title: VLA-JEPA: Enhancing Vision-Language-Action Model with Latent World Model
authors: Jingwen Sun; Wenyao Zhang; Zekun Qi; Shaojie Ren; Zezhi Liu; Hanxin Zhu; Guangzhong Sun; Xin Jin; Zhibo Chen
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2602.10098 in Feb 2026; supplied version dated 17 Feb 2026
open_source_notes: Code, project page, and Hugging Face models are publicly released.
primary_problem: World Modeling; Policy Learning; Human-Video Pretraining; Generalization
world_model_detail: A frozen V-JEPA 2 world-state encoder and causal transformer predictor learn leakage-free latent state transitions conditioned on learned latent-action tokens; a flow-matching head generates robot actions.
action_conditioned: Yes — conditioned on learned latent-action tokens rather than explicit low-level actions during human-video pretraining
video_prediction: No — future states are predicted in V-JEPA latent space
planning_method: None — latent dynamics supervise a direct VLA action policy
horizon_basis: The model predicts an action chunk and latent future-state sequence; it does not search over candidate action sequences. LIBERO includes long-horizon evaluations.
replanning_online: Yes — action trajectories are regenerated from current observations during closed-loop execution
learning_type: Self-Supervised Video Pretraining; Imitation Learning; Flow Matching
policy_type: VLA with JEPA Latent World Model and Flow-Matching Action Head
pretraining: Yes — Qwen3-VL, V-JEPA 2, Something-Something-v2 human video, and DROID robot demonstrations
observation_modality: Language; multi-view RGB video; robot demonstration trajectories
action_space: Continuous action trajectories generated by conditional flow matching
benchmark: LIBERO; LIBERO-Plus; SimplerEnv; three custom real-world tasks
robot_platform: Franka Emika Panda; Google Robot; WidowX
main_contribution: Introduces leakage-free JEPA pretraining that learns action-relevant latent transition semantics from action-free human video and robot demonstrations.
novelty: Future frames are used only as latent supervision targets, avoiding pixel shortcuts and future-information leakage while reducing prior latent-action pipelines to two stages.
limitations: Training uses substantial compute, the real-world evaluation contains only three tasks and 100 demonstrations, performance is sensitive to prediction horizon, and the model provides no explicit planning or counterfactual action search.
future_work: Scale human-video pretraining with more robot data and text-based reasoning signals and improve robustness and generalization.
evidence_world_model: A causal transformer predicts future V-JEPA world-state embeddings from past states and learned latent-action tokens.
evidence_planning: The predicted latent dynamics condition a flow-matching action head; no MPC, search, or candidate-rollout scoring is used.
evidence_horizon: LIBERO long-horizon tasks are evaluated, but the method remains a closed-loop chunked policy with planning_horizon=None.
review_flags: Core JEPA/VLA world-model paper. Count as a strict latent world model but exclude from explicit-planner counts.
source_document: VLA-JEPA_ Enhancing Vision-Language-Action Model with Latent World Model.txt

title: Unfolding the Literature: A Review of Robotic Cloth Manipulation
authors: Alberta Longhini; Yufei Wang; Irene Garcia-Camacho; David Blanco-Mulero; Marco Moletta; Michael Welle; Guillem Alenyà; Hang Yin; Zackory Erickson; David Held; Júlia Borràs; Danica Kragic
year_basis: First published as a Review in Advance on 2 Dec 2024; journal volume year is 2025
venue: Annual Review of Control, Robotics, and Autonomous Systems
open_source_notes: The article is open access under CC BY 4.0, but no companion code repository is identified.
task_domain: Cloth Manipulation; Deformable Objects; Assistive and Industrial Robotics
primary_problem: Survey; Generalization; Modeling; Perception; Manipulation; Benchmarking
long_horizon: N/A — long-horizon adaptive textile manipulation is identified as a grand challenge
human_robot_interaction: N/A — assistive dressing and healthcare applications are reviewed
uses_world_model: N/A — analytical and learned textile dynamics models are reviewed
world_model_family: N/A — multiple analytical, graph, neural, and simulation-based models
world_model_detail: Reviews textile dynamics modeling, perception, manipulation strategies, datasets, and benchmarks with emphasis on variation in physical and mechanical properties.
uses_planning: N/A — planning and control methods are reviewed
planning_representation: Multiple: particles, meshes, keypoints, latent states, and visual/tactile representations
horizon_basis: The review covers individual and sequential cloth tasks but proposes no normalized planner.
observation_modality: Vision; depth; tactile; force; audio; multimodal textile sensing
goal_representation: Multiple cloth configurations and application-specific objectives
action_space: Multiple: grasping, pick-and-place, dynamic manipulation, dressing, folding, and industrial operations
benchmark: Multiple cloth datasets, standardized textile sets, simulators, and manipulation benchmarks
main_contribution: Reviews robotic textile manipulation through the lens of generalization and adaptation to variations in object properties and tasks.
novelty: Connects textile physical and mechanical properties to modeling, perception, manipulation, datasets, and benchmarking rather than organizing only by task.
limitations: The field has limited generalization across textiles, few online adaptation methods, underexplored multimodal sensing and diffusion policies, and weak standardization of datasets and benchmarks.
future_work: Develop property-aware perception, adaptive multitask multimodal agents for long-horizon tasks, and standardized datasets and benchmarks capturing real-world textile variation.
evidence_world_model: The review covers analytical and learned cloth dynamics models but contributes no single predictive world model.
evidence_planning: Planning and control approaches are surveyed as part of a broader modeling-perception-manipulation pipeline.
evidence_horizon: Long-horizon textile autonomy is proposed as a future grand challenge rather than evaluated by one method.
review_flags: Supporting deformable-manipulation survey; retain for cloth, generalization, and benchmark-gap analyses but exclude from method counts.
source_document: Unfolding the Literature_A Review of Robotic Cloth Manipulation.txt

title: Understanding World or Predicting Future? A Comprehensive Survey of World Models
authors: Jingtao Ding; Yunke Zhang; Yu Shang; Jie Feng; Yuheng Zhang; Zefang Zong; Yuan Yuan; Hongyuan Su; Nian Li; Jinghua Piao; Yucheng Deng; Nicholas Sukiennik; Chen Gao; Fengli Xu; Yong Li
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2411.14499 in Nov 2024; supplied revision v4 dated 10 Dec 2025
open_source_notes: The authors maintain a public World-Model repository containing representative papers and code links.
task_domain: General AI; Robotics; Autonomous Driving; Games; Social Simulation
primary_problem: Survey; World-Model Taxonomy; Representation; Future Prediction
long_horizon: N/A — long-term prediction and decision making are reviewed
language_conditioned: N/A — LLM and multimodal world models are reviewed
uses_world_model: N/A — the paper reviews multiple definitions and families
world_model_family: N/A — model-based RL, JEPA, video generation, LLM, and interactive 3D models
world_model_detail: Categorizes world models into implicit representations that understand mechanisms and predictive models that simulate future world evolution.
uses_planning: N/A — decision-making and planning applications are reviewed
planning_representation: Multiple: latent state, language knowledge, pixels/video, 3D environments, and social states
horizon_basis: The survey discusses long-term consistency and planning but does not implement one planner.
observation_modality: Multiple: text; image; video; 3D; driving sensors; agent observations
benchmark: Multiple benchmarks across games, autonomous driving, robotics, embodied environments, and social simulacra
main_contribution: Provides a comprehensive taxonomy separating world understanding through implicit representation from future prediction through simulation.
novelty: Unifies model-based RL, JEPA, LLM world knowledge, video generation, interactive environments, and domain applications under two core functions.
limitations: World models still struggle with causal and counterfactual reasoning, physical consistency, action controllability, long-term coherence, evaluation, scalability, safety, and accountability.
future_work: Develop interactive and physically grounded multimodal world models, stronger long-horizon consistency and controllability, better evaluation, and safeguards for responsible use.
evidence_world_model: The survey explicitly distinguishes internal world representations from models that forecast future external states.
evidence_planning: Planning and decision making are treated as downstream consumers of representation and prediction capabilities.
evidence_horizon: Long-horizon prediction is a field-level challenge rather than a paper-specific planning horizon.
review_flags: Exclude from method counts; retain for taxonomy comparison, definitions, and broad research-gap analysis.
source_document: Understanding World or Predicting Future_ A Comprehensive Survey of World Models.txt

title: Surfer: Progressive Reasoning with World Models for Robotic Manipulation
authors: Pengzhen Ren; Kaidong Zhang; Zixuan Li; Hetao Zheng; Yuhang Wen; Fengda Zhu; Mas Ma; Xiaodan Liang
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2306.11335 in Jun 2023; supplied revision v4 dated 20 Mar 2024
open_source_notes: No public code repository was identified in the supplied manuscript.
primary_problem: World Modeling; Policy Learning; Multimodal Reasoning; Benchmarking
world_model_detail: Transformer action and scene heads jointly predict the next robot action and the latent feature of the next visual frame from image history, robot state, language, and predicted action.
prediction_target: Next action and next-frame visual feature
action_conditioned: Yes — the scene predictor is conditioned on the predicted robot action
video_prediction: No — predicts a single next-frame feature rather than a video rollout
planning_method: None — direct action prediction with auxiliary next-scene supervision
horizon_basis: SeaWave's four levels increase linguistic and visual reasoning difficulty rather than temporal task length.
replanning_online: Yes — the next action is regenerated from recent image and state history
learning_type: Imitation Learning; Auxiliary Latent Scene Prediction
pretraining: Yes — pretrained CLIP vision and text encoders
observation_modality: RGB history; robot state; natural-language instruction
action_space: Continuous 7D end-effector and gripper delta action
benchmark: SeaWave: eight skills, 139 object categories, four progressive-reasoning levels, and 3.4K instructions
main_contribution: Jointly models action execution and resulting visual-scene change to improve language-grounded manipulation generalization.
novelty: Introduces the physically executed SeaWave benchmark with automated scene, instruction, and demonstration generation alongside the action-and-scene predictive model.
limitations: Evaluation is simulation-only, uses a single arm and rigid objects, and lacks force sensing and deformable-object manipulation.
future_work: Extend to dual-arm manipulation, soft objects, force sensing, and richer multimodal inputs.
evidence_world_model: The scene head predicts the next latent visual state conditioned on image history and the predicted action.
evidence_planning: The world-model loss regularizes a direct action predictor; there is no search, model rollout, or candidate-plan scoring.
evidence_horizon: The benchmark tests progressively harder instructions for individual skills, not multi-stage long-horizon manipulation.
review_flags: Strict one-step latent world model, but exclude from explicit-planner counts and do not treat SeaWave reasoning levels as temporal horizon.
source_document: Surfer_ Progressive Reasoning with World Models for Robotic Manipulation.txt

title: Subspace-Decomposed JEPAs: Disentangling Progression and Content in Latent World Models
authors: Lucas Thil; Jesse Read; Rim Kaddah; Guillaume Doquet
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2605.31111 on 29 May 2026
open_source_notes: Code is publicly released at the SD-JEPA repository.
task_domain: General Control; Manipulation; Navigation; Representation Learning
primary_problem: World Modeling; Planning; Interpretable Latent Representation
world_model_detail: Extends LEWM by splitting its learned latent into orthogonal progression and content subspaces, with temporal triplet ordering on progression and SIGReg anti-collapse on content.
planning_representation: Decomposed JEPA latent with content and progression costs
horizon_basis: CEM plans five frame-skipped actions with frame-skip five, corresponding to 25 environment steps, and replans throughout a 50-step evaluation.
learning_type: Self-Supervised Latent World-Model Learning; Temporal Contrastive Learning
policy_type: None — actions are selected by CEM-MPC
pretraining: No external foundation-model pretraining; inherits the end-to-end LEWM encoder-predictor architecture
observation_modality: Pixel observations; actions; benchmark-specific state information
goal_representation: Goal observation encoded in latent space
main_contribution: Separates task progression from content in a JEPA latent and demonstrates that the progression subspace improves planning and provides an interpretable phase coordinate.
novelty: Uses disjoint anti-collapse objectives on orthogonal subspaces and exposes a signed angular progression signal that tracks progress, regression, and semantic perturbations.
limitations: The optimal progression dimension is environment-specific, planning gains are task-dependent, cosine geometry is hand selected, and the orthogonal decomposition is fixed rather than learned.
future_work: Learn the orthogonal decomposition, explore alternative metrics and progression readouts, and investigate broader geometric diagnostics and planning costs.
evidence_world_model: An action-conditioned predictor forecasts the next JEPA latent from observation history and actions.
evidence_planning: CEM samples and scores action sequences using predicted latent distance to the goal, with online receding-horizon replanning.
evidence_horizon: The explicit horizon is 25 environment steps; the benchmark tasks are local control rather than task-level long-horizon manipulation.
review_flags: Core JEPA world-model and planning paper. Classify planning_horizon as Medium while long_horizon remains No.
source_document: Subspace-Decomposed JEPAs_ Disentangling Progression and Content in Latent World Models.txt
